Looking good. Structurally I think it's great, I just think we should change a few variable names and plumb them though.

We'll have to remember to change the ENOS_VAR_... variable to disable aws secrets while we're still investigating why it is having trouble cleaning up.

"This is a nit, but I wanted to point it out anyway: does the nested directory here add any value? It feels like it just adds unnecessary nesting when there's only one file. AWS is the only one currently in its own folder, and honestly that never made sense to me either.

title: Backport of Fix bug preventing multiline statemtents in rotation_statements for database static roles into release/1.20.x
body:
## Backport

This PR is auto-generated from #31442 to be assessed for backporting due to the inclusion of the label backport/1.20.x.



The below text is copied from the body of the original PR.

---

## Description
Fixes https://github.com/hashicorp/vault/issues/31369

The `rotation_statements` field for Postgres static roles does not currently support multi-line sql statements. This PR adds support for them.

## Manual Testing

### Replication of Bug

* Create vault server with database secrets engine enabled
* Create postgres secrets engine config

```
vault write database/config/postgres \
    plugin_name=postgresql-database-plugin \
    allowed_roles="example_static" \
    connection_url="postgresql://{{username}}:{{password}}@localhost:5432/mydatabase?sslmode=disable" \
    username="my_username" \
    password="super_secure_password"
```
* Confirmation of bug after attempting to add a multi-line sql statement to a Postgres static role config. 

```
vault write database/static-roles/example_static \
    db_name=postgres \
    username=example_static \
    rotation_period=3600 \
    rotation_statements="DO \$\$ BEGIN IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT FROM pg_catalog.pg_roles WHERE rolname='{{name}}') THEN CREATE ROLE \"{{name}}\" WITH LOGIN PASSWORD '{{password}}'; ELSE ALTER ROLE \"{{name}}\" WITH LOGIN PASSWORD '{{password}}'; END IF; END \$\$"
Error writing data to database/static-roles/example_static: Error making API request.

URL: PUT http://127.0.0.1:8200/v1/database/static-roles/example_static
Code: 500. Errors:

* 1 error occurred:
        * error setting credentials: 1 error occurred:
        * failed to execute query: ERROR: unterminated dollar-quoted string at or near "$$ BEGIN IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT FROM pg_catalog.pg_roles WHERE rolname='example_static') THEN CREATE ROLE "example_static" WITH LOGIN PASSWORD '9nZM--Bzd3NJDe79140h'" (SQLSTATE 42601)
```

### Demonstration that Bug was Resolved

#### when rotation_statements contains a password
```
vault write database/static-roles/my-static-role \
    db_name=my-postgresql-database \
    username="app_user" \
    rotation_period="24h" \
    rotation_statements="DO \$\$ BEGIN IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT FROM pg_catalog.pg_roles WHERE rolname='{{name}}') THEN CREATE ROLE \"{{name}}\" WITH LOGIN PASSWORD '{{password}}'; ELSE ALTER ROLE \"{{name}}\" WITH LOGIN PASSWORD '{{password}}'; END IF; END \$\$"
Success! Data written to: database/static-roles/my-static-role
```

#### when rotation_statements contains an expiration
```
vault write database/static-roles/my-static-role \
    db_name=my-postgresql-database \
    username="app_user" \
    rotation_period="24h" \
rotation_statements="DO \$\$ BEGINALTER ROLE \"{{name}}\" VALID UNTIL '{{expiration}}'; END \$\$"
Success! Data written to: database/static-roles/my-static-role
```

title: UI Bug fix: Fix DR Secondary view
body: - [x] Ent tests pass ### Description The DR secondary view failed to load after PR #30425. The error occurred because of the addition of the `async` keyword to the `afterModel` hook. My best guess as to why this breaks the DR Secondary transition is that `async` causes Ember to wait for any awaited promises before completing the route transition. For most routes, this is fine, but DR secondary is sensitive to timing, and the transition hangs/fails silently, breaking the transition. _Note: The addition of `async` on the `afterModel` hook does not cause a failure for Performance Secondaries._ To repro: - Confirming it was the Posthog pr: checkout commit `d7bb0adfe0` (the commit right before the Posthog PR), create a DR secondary, and navigate to the UI view. Then, check out the Posthog commit `689ede2da5` and follow the same steps. It works on the commit before, and fails on the regression commit. - To reproduce the fix: on this PR, add the `async` before the `afterModel` hook on the cluster route, and the DR secondary will fail to transition. Remove `async`, and it will transition. This shows that it's not the `this.analytics` service check that's breaking the flow, it's the `async` keyword added to the `afterModel` hook. Failure: After fix:

Unwittingly, this also removed a very useful feature in development: if you run `yarn start` (which runs `ember server`), you can then navigate to `http://localhost:4200/ui/tests` and get the QUnit UI with a fully running tests suite. We released Audit Log Filtering in Vault 1.16.0. It appeared both in 1.16.0 and 1.17.0, so I've removed it from the 1.17.0 features list.

title: Backport of Fix missing dependencies in enos workflow for raft node removal into release/1.20.x
body:
## Backport

This PR is auto-generated from #31446 to be assessed for backporting due to the inclusion of the label backport/1.20.x.



The below text is copied from the body of the original PR.

---

### Description

This PR fixes missing dependencies in the enos workflow during raft node removal operations. After removing a raft node, we run an unseal test that is expected to fail, but we were not requiring this test to complete before re-adding the node back to the cluster. This could cause race conditions where nodes are re-added before we've properly validated the expected unseal failure.

The fix adds the missing test dependency to ensure the unseal failure test runs and completes before proceeding with node re-addition. This dependency was previously missing but is necessary for reliable test sequencing and proper validation of the expected behavior.

## Backport

This PR is auto-generated from #23453 to be assessed for backporting due to the inclusion of the label backport/1.14.x.

The below text is copied from the body of the original PR.

---

In #22817, we've introduced a CI bug where we started checking out the `github.base_ref` instead of the `github.ref`, which meant the tests were running against the target branch of the pull request, so not testing the changes.

From the docs: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/contexts

`github.base_ref`  
The base_ref or target branch of the pull request in a workflow run. This property is only available when the event that triggers a workflow run is either pull_request or pull_request_target.

`github.ref`
The fully-formed ref of the branch or tag that triggered the workflow run. For workflows triggered by push, this is the branch or tag ref that was pushed. For workflows triggered by pull_request, this is the pull request merge branch.
